name: 1611 Golf Club
address: 6001 Parker Blvd, North Richland Hills, TX 76180
about: 1611 Golf Club is a premier indoor golf venue located in the Dallas-Fort Worth area. It combines country club vibes with sports bar energy and a social club atmosphere. The venue utilizes cutting-edge Trackman technology for golf simulation, along with full bar and craft kitchen services. It is designed for both serious practice sessions and social gatherings, offering features like wall-to-wall TVs and space for events, parties, and leagues. Bay rentals are available for non-members, with memberships offering perks like daily sim time. The facility can host up to 6 people per bay, though 4 players are recommended for optimal experience, with a 2-hour minimum for all events.
why_golfers_like_it: Professional-grade Trackman technology for accurate ball and club data
Full bar and craft kitchen available for a complete social experience
Flexible booking options with both pay-per-play and membership models
Social atmosphere with wall-to-wall TVs, suitable for groups and events
Expert lessons and coaching available for skill improvement

food_and_drink.notes: Craft kitchen with a full menu available onsite.
Full-service bar with alcoholic beverages available.

simulator_hardware.notes: Advanced radar technology trusted by top golf professionals for precise ball tracking.

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: Most customers bring their own clubs, but rental sets are available for $10 if needed. Club rentals are free for 1611 members.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: The venue has a full bar and craft kitchen available on-site. BYOB policies are not mentioned.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The typical round takes an average golfer 1 hour. It's recommended to add an hour for each player in your group.

faq.question: Is this venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: The website does not specify if the venue is kid-friendly or has special amenities for children.
